chapter.subject: mathematics
chapter.title: Statistics
chapter.studySummary:
## Syllabus outline
- Mean of grouped frequency data (Step-deviation)
- Median of grouped frequency data
- Mode of grouped frequency data
- Empirical relation between measures

Pedagogical concepts and learning benchmarks for Class 10 active curriculum.

videos.title: Statistics Chapter 14 One Shot Class 10 Maths

transcripts.text: Mean: Direct method, assumed mean method, step deviation method.
Mode = l + [(f1 - f0)/(2f1 - f0 - f2)] * h. Modal class has highest frequency.
Median = l + [(n/2 - cf)/f] * h. Cumulative frequency cf is from preceding class.
Empirical relationship: Mode = 3 Median - 2 Mean. Re-checked in board MCQs constantly!

scholarTips: The 'cf' (Cumulative Frequency) term used in Median formula is ALWAYS the cumulative frequency of the CLASS PRECEDING the median class! Double check this!
Equation holds: 3 Median = Mode + 2 Mean. Easy to remember like alphabetical order: Me-di-an (six letters, multiplied by 3), Mo-de (four letters, multiplied by 1).
chapter.disclaimer: AI-generated study guide (VOLTAIC). Not official CBSE/NCERT. Verify facts with your textbook and teacher.
